IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We have identified that a piece of electrical safety equipment called a cut-out needs replacing because the equipment is coming to the end of its serviceable life. This equipment is the link between our electricity network and a property's internal wires. Once complete, the work will ensure that the property is connected safely to our electricity network. We appreciate that some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off and replace the cut-out. We will remove the existing one and install a new one on the same day. This work is taking place near Newtimber Place Lane.

This work is taking place near Drove Way. Temporary 15 minute closure boards will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We regularly inspect our electrical equipment to ensure it is safe and efficient. We need to examine a transformer on our electricity poles to check it is functioning properly. This essential work helps to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned power cuts in the future. To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your electricity. We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network in the future as we appreciate you may have been impacted by an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Fakenham Road.

A lot of our customers have questions regarding power cuts, and though you can always reach our team, you can also find some helpful information and answers to some of our most frequently asked questions on our website here - https://UKPN.short.gy/FAQ - Bear in mind this will use your mobile data, so feel free to speak to us if you'd prefer. We'll be in touch again once we have more information from our staff.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 22:50. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
Steps.Name: We are restoring customers automatically

Steps.Message: We have engineers on their way. As this is affecting your area, they will not need to attend your property. Rest assured once they arrive, they’ll work as quickly as is safely possible to get your power back on.
Steps.Name: Engineers on their way

Steps.Message: Engineers are now on site, carrying out investigations into the fault. Once they have completed their assessment, they will carry out repairs.
Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

Steps.Message: All power in the area should be back on. We’re really sorry for any disruption this caused.
Steps.Name: Power back on

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We were investigating a high voltage overhead electricity line fault which caused a power cut in the local area.

Logs.Comment:
Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 709 customers in the Norwich, NR10 3 and NR12 7 area by removing a fallen tree from the network and repairing the overhead cabling.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve the issue safely. We apologise the outage lasted longer than anticipated and we appreciate your understanding.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by Aaron at 10:19

We're sorry you've been impacted by this, and we'll provide you an update as soon as possible. Sent by Aaron at 10:09.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have now confirmed that we have a tree on our overhead network and also some faulty cabling that needs repairing. Due to the work needed, its likely this will need to be passed to a relief team to attend first thing in the morning to complete.

Due to the time it could take for them to attend and repair the fault, we're now estimating to have the power back on between 09:30 and 10:30. We're sorry this will take longer than we had anticipated. If you have any further questions or concerns though then don't hesitate to contact us free on 105.

We'll update you again when we have any further information. Sent by Will at 04:29.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Will at 02:15 - Our engineers have been able to divert electricity around the faulty part of the network, restoring power to another 19 customers. If your power is still affected, our engineers have been in touch to advise they are still working to divert power around the network to try and restore supplies but this is taking longer than anticipated. Due to this, we're now estimating that we'll have the remaining power restored by 03:30 and 04:30. We're very sorry to adjust our time frame, but we'll continue to keep you updated.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 01:26 - Thanks for your patience today. Our engineers have now investigated the reports of a grounded overhead cable, and have deemed it safe to restore power back to all 232 properties in the Norwich, NR10 3 and 5 area.

We're very sorry we had to turn your power off unexpectedly, and with so little warning. We usually provide much more notice if we can, but in a situation like this our first priority is the safety of the public and our staff.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. If your power is still off or if you have any questions, call our team on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

We have been alerted to a potentially dangerous situation involving a grounded overhead cable, so have responded as quickly as possible and asked our control room to turn power off to the area using our high voltage network. This is why a wide area has lost electricity.

Once further specialist field staff have assessed the situation, we'll start diverting power back to affected homes as soon as they advise us it's safe. You may notice your lights going on and off as we work to get as much power restored as we can.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 00:10 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Norwich, NR10 3 and 5 area in the interest of the public's health and safety. This is so they can investigate reports of an overhead cable that is on the ground.

If in the area, please take care and stay at least 10m away from the cable. 

Until our engineers arrive on site and investigate, it's difficult to advise you a time frame. As soon as this becomes available, we'll let you know. We'll keep you updated with our progress, but in the meantime our team are always here to talk on 105 if you have any questions or concerns.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Aaron at 06:10 - Our engineers attended earlier and conformed the issue is an overhead network fault. Specialist teams are now on the way, to repair the cabling.

Engineers from our control room are also working to divert power remotely, so it's possible your power could come back on at any point. We can't always restore everyone this way. That's why we're also sending staff to the area.

Every fault is different, so our time frames have to be estimated but currently we hope to have power back on between 07:30 and 08:30. We must stress that it may become necessary for this to change, depending on what our engineers find.

We'll keep you updated with our progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 01:46 - Thanks for bearing with us tonight.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Sent by Lee at 11:33 - I'm texting to say I am glad to report that power to the George Street TN9 2 area should now be back on.

Following thorough tests at our substation and on local network equipment, our staff have been able to safely replace our network equipment. The suspected underground cabling issue is not yet appearing on any of our tests, so for the moment we have restored your power as quickly as possible.

I'm afraid this may happen again, but every time you lose power it gives us a better chance of pinpointing the problem. I'm sorry for the continued disruption, but If you'd like to talk to us about this, or if your power isn't on call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
